finding: The oracle accepts its complete control and rejects all 24 permanent fake-fleet or missing-evidence mutations.

reviewers.finding: Completion after proof but before preview needed an independent mutation.
Unknown event types were accepted despite the fail-closed contract.
The fixed 19-mutation oracle supports its narrow claim.
Provider/model mutation 20 passed, but findings.json still claimed 19 mutations.
Mutation 20 is consistent, 20/20 turn red, and the failed 001 run remains non-qualifying.
A checked candidate exceeded its sealed one-million-token ceiling; budget enforcement was missing from the oracle.
Infinity bypassed sealed budget comparisons because numeric fields were not required to be finite.
Unsafe worker counts were not rejected directly and negative event clocks could admit negative elapsed time.
The isolated-loop oracle rejects all 24 mutations and keeps wefbw9 non-qualifying.

limits: No real model fleet is measured.
The mutation corpus is permanent but not complete.
The active local MCP process still uses the startup heartbeat wrapper until it reloads the public Terrarium fix.
next: Build the strongest frozen single-agent baseline; do not start the eight-agent tier.
